title: Bridge Essay: What are the neural correlates of meta-cognition and anosognosia in Alzheimer's

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition — self-monitoring, metacognitive awareness, prefrontal-parietal insight circuits.

**What are the neural correlates of meta-cognition and anosognosia in Alzheimer'sdisease? Asystematic review.**. Neurobiol Aging.. PMID: 32679396. DOI: 10.1016/j.neurobiolaging.2020.06.011. Awareness of one'sown cognitive processes (metacognition) or of one'sown illness or deficits (anosognosia) can be impaired in people with Alzheimer'sdisease (AD). The neural correlates of anosognosia within AD remain inconclusive. Understanding anosognosia is of importance because of its impact on carer burden and increased institutionalization.
